title: Rathnagiriswarar Temple,Thirumarugal
tagline: Rathnagiriswarar Temple,Thirumarugal — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Rathnagiriswarar Temple (Tamil: இரத்தினகிரிஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, located in Thirumarugal, 10 km East of Nannilam, Near Nagapatnam, in Nagapattinam di
overview.description: Rathnagiriswarar Temple (Tamil: இரத்தினகிரிஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, located in Thirumarugal, 10 km East of Nannilam, Near Nagapatnam, in Nagapattinam district, Tamil Nadu in India. It is one of the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalams, where two of the most revered Nayanars (Saivite Saints), Appar and Tirugnana Sambandar have sung the glories of this temple.

topPlaces.name: Tiruchenkattankudi Uthirapasupatheeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Tiruchenkattankudi Uthirapasupatheeswarar Temple ( Tamil: திருச்செங்காட்டங்குடி உத்தராபதீசுவரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple located at Tiruchengattankudi in Nagapattinam District, Tamil Nadu, India. The historical name of the place is Ganapatheesaram.

topPlaces.name: Uthrapathiswaraswamy Temple
topPlaces.description: Uthrapathiswaraswamy Temple is a Hindu temple in Tiruchenkattankudi in Nagapattinam district in the Tamil Nadu state of India. Though it is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva, it is more famous for its Ganesha (Ganapati) icons.

topPlaces.name: Vizhithiyur
topPlaces.description: Vizhithiyur is a village in Puducherry, India. It is one of the three revenue villages in Neravy Taluk, Karaikal district.

topPlaces.name: Ramanathaswami Temple, Tiruvarur
topPlaces.description: Ramanathaswami Temple (Tamil: திருக்கண்ணபுரம் இராமனதீசுவரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple located at Thirukannapuram in the Tiruvarur district of Tamil Nadu, India. Dedicated to Shiva, the temple is located in the village of Tirukannapuram between Tiruvarur and Mayiladuthurai.

topPlaces.name: Neelamegha Perumal Temple
topPlaces.description: The Neelamegha Perumal Temple or Sowriraja Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple in Thirukannapuram, a village in the outskirts of Nagapattinam in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Divya Prabandha, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desam dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Neelamegha Perumal (also called as Neela Meghar) and his consort Lakshmi as Thirukannapura Nayagi.

topPlaces.name: Thirukannapuram
topPlaces.description: Thirukannapuram is a village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, Nagapattinam District. A popular Perumal temple (Neelamegha Perumal temple) is there.

topPlaces.name: Polagam, Karaikal
topPlaces.description: Polagam is a revenue village in the Karaikal taluk of Karaikal District. It is situated at a distance of about 8 kilometres south-west of Karaikal town and 2 kilometres south of Tirumalarajanpattinam commune.

topPlaces.name: Agnipureeswarar Temple, Thirupugalur
topPlaces.description: Agnipureeswarar Temple (also called Thirupugalur temple) in Thirupugalur, a village in Nagapattinam district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva. Constructed in the Tamil style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the Cholas period in the 10th century.

Vizhithiyur is a village in Puducherry, India. It is one of the three revenue villages in Neravy Taluk, Karaikal district.

Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Divya Prabandha, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desam dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Neelamegha Perumal (also called as Neela Meghar) and his consort Lakshmi as Thirukannapura Nayagi.

Agnipureeswarar Temple (also called Thirupugalur temple) in Thirupugalur, a village in Nagapattinam district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva. Constructed in the Tamil style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the Cholas period in the 10th century.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rathnagiriswarar Temple,Thirumarugal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rathnagiriswarar Temple,Thirumarugal is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rathnagiriswarar Temple,Thirumarugal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (114 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (116 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rathnagiriswarar Temple,Thirumarugal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rathnagiriswarar Temple,Thirumarugal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rathnagiriswarar Temple,Thirumarugal?
faq.a: Stays in Rathnagiriswarar Temple,Thirumarugal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rathnagiriswarar Temple,Thirumarugal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tiruchenkattankudi Uthirapasupatheeswarar Temple, Uthrapathiswaraswamy Temple, Vizhithiyur, Ramanathaswami Temple, Tiruvarur, Neelamegha Perumal Temple.
